fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Maryland Dental Assistant License Requirements - How to Get Certified (2026)
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Maryland — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license maryland, dental assistant certification maryland, RDA maryland, DANB CDA maryland, dental assistant radiography maryland

board.name: Maryland State Board of Dental Examiners

license_types.title: Registered Dental Assistant (RDA)
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Maryland.

steps.title: Complete Education/Training
steps.description: Complete a CODA-accredited dental assisting program or gain on-the-job training under a licensed dentist.

steps.title: Obtain Required Certifications
steps.description: Obtain DANB CDA certification or pass state-specific exams as required.

steps.title: Apply for State Credential
steps.description: Submit application to the Maryland State Board of Dental Examiners with required documentation and fees.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the Maryland State Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

faqs.question: Does Maryland require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Maryland requires Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Maryland?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Maryland.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Maryland?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Maryland?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $200. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the Maryland State Board of Dental Examiners by registration.
